raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I reported three fraudulent charges on my Citi credit card ending in XXXX : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$48.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$60.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$60.00} After I reported the fraud, I received provisional credits and the card was closed. Citi then issued me a replacement card ending in XXXX. 

Once I activated the new card, these same three fraudulent charges appeared again on the replacement account for whatever reason XXXX $ less per transaction. Because of this, my account now shows a balance of {$170.00} for transactions that I did not make and have already reported as fraud.

Over the past month, I have called Citi customer service three separate times trying to get this resolved. Each time, I was told that I am not responsible for these charges. The problem is that the balance is still on my account. I have also tried submitting disputes through both the Citi mobile app and the website, but nothing has been corrected. I have also emailed them at XXXX hoping to get a resolution as well.

At this point, it has been more than a month since I first reported these charges. I have done everything I can on my end, and I should not have to continue spending time following up on fraudulent transactions that were already reported and credited once before.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to a prompt resolution.
